dueDatePage.title: Due Date Calculator
dueDatePage.subtitle: Estimate when your baby will arrive
dueDatePage.lmpLabel: First Day of Last Menstrual Period
dueDatePage.info: Only about 5% of babies are born on their exact due date. Most are born within 2 weeks before or after. Your healthcare provider may adjust your due date based on ultrasound measurements.

kickCounterPage.title: Kick Counter
kickCounterPage.subtitle: Count 10 kicks to monitor fetal activity
kickCounterPage.goal: Goal: 10 kicks in 2 hours or less
kickCounterPage.info: Starting at 28 weeks, count fetal movements daily. Contact your healthcare provider if you notice decreased movement or don't reach 10 kicks in 2 hours.

contractionPage.title: Contraction Timer
contractionPage.subtitle: Track duration and frequency of contractions
contractionPage.callProviderDesc: Contractions are 5 minutes apart and lasting 45+ seconds. Consider heading to the hospital.
contractionPage.rule511Desc: Call your provider when contractions are 5 minutes apart, lasting 1 minute each, for at least 1 hour.

waterPage.title: Water Intake Tracker
waterPage.subtitle: Stay hydrated during pregnancy — aim for 10+ glasses daily

hospitalBagPage.title: Hospital Bag Checklist
hospitalBagPage.info: Pack your bag around 36 weeks. Keep it somewhere easy to grab, and make sure your partner knows where it is!

conceptionPage.info: Conception typically occurs around ovulation, 14 days before your next expected period. The fertile window spans 5 days before and 1 day after ovulation.

pregnancyDiaryPage.title: Pregnancy Diary
pregnancyDiaryPage.notePlaceholder: Write about your day, feelings, or memories...

forbiddenFoodsPage.title: Foods to Avoid
forbiddenFoodsPage.disclaimer: This is a general guide. Always consult your healthcare provider about dietary restrictions specific to your pregnancy.

reasons.rawFishReason: May contain parasites and bacteria harmful to baby
reasons.rawMeatReason: Risk of toxoplasmosis and E. coli infection
reasons.deliReason: Risk of listeria unless heated until steaming
reasons.mercuryReason: High mercury levels can harm baby's development
reasons.sproutsReason: Bacteria can grow in sprout seeds
reasons.herbalTeaReason: Some herbs may be unsafe - check with doctor
reasons.liverReason: Very high in Vitamin A which can harm baby
reasons.tunaReason: Contains moderate mercury - limit to 2-3 portions weekly
reasons.sweetenersReason: Some may cross placenta - use in moderation
reasons.cookedFishReason: Great source of omega-3 for baby's brain
reasons.pasteurizedReason: Safe and good source of calcium
reasons.cookedMeatReason: Safe source of protein and iron
reasons.hardCheeseReason: Made with pasteurized milk, safe to eat

genderPredictorPage.title: Gender Predictor
genderPredictorPage.disclaimer: This is for entertainment purposes only. The only accurate way to determine baby's gender is through medical testing like ultrasound or genetic screening.
genderPredictorPage.funNote: This is just for fun! Based on the ancient Chinese Gender Prediction Chart - not scientifically proven but entertaining to try!
genderPredictorPage.resultNote: Remember, this is just for fun! Only medical tests can confirm your baby's gender.

preeclampsiaPage.title: Preeclampsia Risk Assessment
preeclampsiaPage.subtitle: Check your preeclampsia risk factors
preeclampsiaPage.disclaimer: This is a preliminary assessment only. Always consult your healthcare provider.

fitnessPage.title: AI Fitness Coach
fitnessPage.disclaimer: Consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program.

cravingsPage.whyCraving: Why you might be craving this

nutrition.addFoodFirst: Add at least one food item
nutrition.logMealFirst: Log at least one meal first
nutrition.weekInfo: Week {{week}} - Log your meals and get AI tips
nutrition.tip1: Eat 5-6 small meals instead of 3 large ones
nutrition.tip3: Avoid raw foods and unpasteurized cheeses
nutrition.tip4: Focus on protein, iron, and calcium
